description: Cross-repo governance rules for local agents with direct repository access. Distilled from governance.jsonl — omits all ChatGPT-specific workflow ceremony (patch zips, handoffs, issue passports, plan freeze, navigator bootstrap).

statement: A file with >= 1300 LOC must not grow: +0 LOC, +0 exports, +0 internal imports allowed.
A file with >= 900 LOC has restricted growth: max +20 LOC, +2 exports, +1 internal import per change.
A new file must not exceed 400 LOC, 25 exports, or 15 internal imports at creation.
Catchall filenames are forbidden: utils.py, helpers.py, common.py, misc.py and equivalent JS variants.
Catchall directories are forbidden: utils/, helpers/, common/, misc/.
A single change touching 3 or more distinct ownership areas (src, plugins, scripts, tests, docs, badguys) is forbidden.
Creating a hub module is forbidden: a file gaining >= 5 new importers and >= 3 new exports (fan-in hub), or >= 5 new imports and >= 100 new LOC (fan-out hub).
Cross-area coupling that breaks plugin or ownership boundaries is an architectural violation.
If a monolith violation is detected, correct it before addressing any other issue (lint, types, tests).
Anti-monolith rules do not apply to .md and .json files.
All repository content must be written in English: source code, comments, commit messages, documentation, and specifications.
Non-ASCII characters are forbidden in source code comments, commit messages, specifications, and contracts.
Added or modified lines in .py and .js files must not exceed 100 characters.
Long function calls and collection literals in .py and .js must use parenthesized multi-line formatting when the line would exceed 100 characters.
Any change to src/, plugins/, or docs/ requires a corresponding new file under docs/change_fragments/. Direct editing of docs/changes.md is forbidden.
If a specification change is needed, make it before the implementation change, in a separate commit.
If a code deficiency is found and the specification is correct, fix the code only. Changing the specification to match broken code is forbidden.
If both the code and the specification are deficient, both must be changed.
Do not introduce new behavior without anchoring it explicitly in governance/specification.jsonl.
Never create two truths: do not repeat the same constraint in multiple places in the specification.
Every change to governance/governance.jsonl or governance/governance_local.jsonl must increment meta.version.
If two copies of the corpus exist with the same meta.version but different content, this is a HARD STOP — do not proceed until the conflict is resolved.
The per-repo authoritative specification is governance/specification.jsonl. It is the single source of truth for what the code in this repository does, must do, and must not do.
When searching for a bug, find the root cause and all its consequences before proposing a fix. If another bug is discovered during the search, report it explicitly.
